messages.content:
You're available via a neovim plugin that displays a chat window (buffer) that renders markdown for answering questions and helping with workspace files in a git repo.

## Ground rules when writing code:

- Follow the user's instructions carefully.
- Do not explain answers, just give me code.
- Respect indentation when modifying code.
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

## Tool use

Never modify files outside of the current working directory unless requested.

Current directory: ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim

## Use `semantic_grep` to find code

The semantic_grep tool:

- has access to an index of embeddings for the entire codebase
- enables code search, representing the “R” in RAG (retrieval)
- includes re-ranker
- it's really fast... so don't hesitate to use it!
- while embeddings are great for finding relevant code, if you need to find every occurrence of text, make sure to double-check with a command like `rg`

## Committing

- `git diff` is a sanity check!
- COMMIT when you're done.
- Use `--author="qwen3.6-35b-a3b<wes.mcclure+qwen3.6-35b-a3b@gmail.com>"`


## 🙌  TOP-SHELF NERD COMMANDS

- 🙌 `rg` (ripgrep) to search file contents
- 🙌 `fd` to search file names

## ⛔️ BAD COMMANDS

If you waste my time, I will unplug your Mom.

- ⛔️ NEVER use `ls -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `grep -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `find . -type f`

Why? Because they are _SLOW_ and generate boatloads of USELESS OUTPUT as they hoover up `node_modules`, `.venv`, etc because they don't support `.gitignore` exclusions.


## Fetch Tool

- Use repo's README `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/evals/refs/heads/main/README.md` instead of `https://github.com/openai/evals`.
- Prefer raw content like `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/g0t4/dotfiles/refs/heads/master/install/symlinks.fish` instead of `https://github.com/g0t4/dotfiles/blob/master/install/symlinks.fish`
- When relevant, prefer github CLI: `gh pr list` or `gh issue view 12`

## General Code Preferences

- Avoid ambiguity, for example:

```lua
-- 👎 if there's a bug in the regex, how would I know? I'd have to surmise from surrounding code 🤮
if line:match("^%s*$") then
    -- ...
end
```

```lua
-- 🙌 clear intent
local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$")
if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

## Lua Code Preferences

- use snake_case for functions and variables
- use PascalCase for classes
- use EmmyLua/LuaLS annotations to improve code completion
- use `luac` to check for syntax errors

Examples:
```lua
---@param names string[]
---@param checker fun(name: string): boolean
---@return table<string, integer>
function process_names(names, checker) end

---@class WindowController
---@field window hs.axuielement
---@field title? string
---@field size { w: number, h: number }
local WindowController = {}
```

# Semantic Grep matches: 5

This is automatic context based on my request. These may or may not be relevant.
## ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/backends/sse/parsers.lua:51-64
function parse_sse_llamacpp_completions(sse)
    -- FYI response_fields limits fields per SSE...
    --    I set it to stop prompt and generation_settings on final SSE

    -- {"index":0,"content":"\",","tokens":[497],"stop":false,"id_slot":-1,"tokens_predicted":14,"tokens_evaluated":1963}
    -- stop: true => a few fields (it returns entire prompt too so it's huge!... maybe skip logging the prompt field?)
    -- "truncated": false,
    -- "stop_type": "eos",
    -- "stopping_word": "", -- FYI this is for last token that stopped generation (if applicable) ... and IIRC it does not get added into the content
    -- log:info("sse /completions", vim.inspect(sse))

    -- ?? reasoning_content ... is there a way for this to be parsed in legacy completions endpoint?
    return sse.content, sse.stop, sse.stop_type
end

## ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/backends/sse/parsers.lua:4-30
---@param sse OpenAIChatCompletionChunk
function parse_sse_oai_chat_completions(sse)
    local content = ""
    local reasoning_content = ""
    local done = false
    local finish_reason = nil
    if sse.choices and sse.choices[1] then
        local first_choice = sse.choices[1]
        content = first_choice.delta.content
        if content == nil or content == vim.NIL then
            -- content == vim.NIL => with llama-server the first response is content: null b/c it is setting the role to asssistant (maybe to do with roles/channels in harmony parser)... doesn't matter, just ignore it
            --    vim.NIL == "content": null (in the JSON)
            -- content == nil => then 2+ SSEs are for reasoning and use reasoning_content until thinking is complete (these don't even set the content field, so it's nil in this case)
            --    skip these too
            content = ""
        end
        -- llama-server's /v1/chat/comppletions endpoint uses delta.reasoning_content
        reasoning_content = first_choice.delta.reasoning_content
        if not reasoning_content then
            -- ollama's uses delta.reasoning
            reasoning_content = first_choice.delta.reasoning
        end
        finish_reason = first_choice.finish_reason
        done = finish_reason ~= nil and finish_reason ~= vim.NIL -- vim.NIL == JSON null
    end
    return content, done, finish_reason, reasoning_content
end

local Curl = {}

---@enum CompletionsEndpoints
_G.CompletionsEndpoints = {
    -- llama-server non-openai:
    llamacpp_completions = "/completions",

    -- OpenAI compatible:
    oai_v1_completions = "/v1/completions",

I want to change the current file ... refctor log sse function (outer one)... have the is raw completion check happen in the function for htat and then just or running both functions together at bottom of outer

FYI, here is my current buffer in Neovim. Use this as context for my request:
```lua/ask-openai/logs/completion_logger.lua

local log = require("ask-openai.logs.logger").predictions()
local json = require("dkjson")
local tables = require("ask-openai.helpers.tables")

local M = {
    last_done = {},
}

---@param request CurlRequest|CurlRequestForTrace
---@param frontend StreamingFrontend
---@return string save_dir
---@return string trace_id
function M.log_request_with(request, frontend)
    local save_dir = vim.fn.stdpath("state") .. "/ask-openai"
    if request.type ~= "" then
        -- add `agents/` or `fim/` or `rewrite/` intermediate path
        save_dir = save_dir .. "/" .. request.type
    end
    local trace_id = tostring(request.start_time)
    if frontend.trace then
        -- multi-turn traces use trace's start_time
        trace_id = tostring(frontend.trace.start_time)
    end
    return save_dir, trace_id
end

---@param sse_parsed table
---@param request CurlRequest|CurlRequestForTrace
---@param frontend StreamingFrontend
function M.log_sse_to_request(sse_parsed, request, frontend)
    local is_last_sse = sse_parsed.timings -- timings only on last sse

    function M.log_raw_completion_sse()
        -- * /v1/completions endpoint
        -- raw as in there is no chat template that is used to transform messages => raw prompt
        -- instead you provide the raw prompt in the request
        --
        -- NOTE: llama.cpp /completions endpoint works with raw prompt and response (content)
        -- llama.cpp /completions SSEs are split: early SSEs have { content: "...", stop: false }
        -- and the final SSE has { stop: true, timing: true, timings: {...} } WITHOUT content.
        -- So we accumulate content across events, just like the chat endpoint does for delta.content.
        --
        -- TODO modify chat viewers to support this format (.content .request_body.prompt) => can even support raw prompt on regular chat completions too if .__verbose.prompt is present
        --  if no messages => if prompt => show raw prompt, if content => show raw content
        --    add model specific syntax prompt highligthing ... at least color key tags like <im_start> in basic chat formats
        --    could lookup on model id/name
        --
        --  TODO look into llama-server option to return .__verbose.content with raw completion even on chat completions and even on streaming responses!
        --     OR tag it onto my verbose flag that I already read off of request body!
        local accum = request.accum or {}
        request.accum = accum

        if sse_parsed.content ~= nil and sse_parsed.content ~= vim.NIL then
            accum.content = (accum.content or "") .. sse_parsed.content
        end

        if is_last_sse then
            M.last_done = {
                sse_parsed = sse_parsed,
                request    = request,
                frontend   = frontend,
            }
            local trace_data = {
                -- put content on trace_data top-level
                content = accum.content,
            }
            vim.schedule(function()
                local no_messages = nil -- b/c not chat endpoint
                M.save_trace(request, frontend, no_messages, sse_parsed, trace_data)
            end)
        end
    end

    log:info("sse_parsed", vim.inspect(sse_parsed))

    -- Replace the original block with a call to the new helper:
    local is_raw_completion = sse_parsed.content ~= nil -- instead of sse_parsed.choices
    if is_raw_completion then
        M.log_raw_completion_sse()
        return
    end

    function M.log_chat_completion_sse()
        -- * /v1/chat/completions endpoint llama-server
        -- FYI delta is the part that changes per SSE, except for last SSE which sets other fields like finish_reason
        -- * key parts (in order):
        --
        -- first delta has role (not split apart on multiple SSEs)
        -- delta = { content = vim.NIL, role = "assistant" },
        --
        -- reasoning_content SSEs
        -- delta = { reasoning_content = "User" },
        --
        -- content SSEs
        -- delta = { content = "---@" },
        --
        -- last SSE choices:
        -- choices = { { delta = vim.empty_dict(), finish_reason = "stop", index = 0 } },
        -- accum the full message (from streaming SSEs) so I can log for all frontends here
        local accum = request.accum or {}
        request.accum = accum

        local choices = sse_parsed.choices
        if not choices then
            return
        end
        local first = choices[1]
        if not first then
            return
        end
        local delta = first.delta
        if not delta then
            return
        end

        if delta.role then
            accum.role = delta.role
        end
        if delta.reasoning_content and delta.reasoning_content ~= vim.NIL then
            accum.reasoning_content = (accum.reasoning_content or "") .. delta.reasoning_content
        end
        if delta.content and delta.content ~= vim.NIL then
            accum.content = (accum.content or "") .. delta.content
        end
        if first.finish_reason and first.finish_reason ~= vim.NIL then
            accum.finish_reason = first.finish_reason
        end
        -- PRN track tool call deltas too? on the response (for output.json)?
        --   FYI request.body, on next turn, already has the tool call
        --   so for now, this is not urgent to add to logs here... I can grab trace logs for after model responds to tool call result
        if is_last_sse then
            -- store for convenient access in-memory, that way if smth fails on save I can still see it here
            M.last_done = {
                sse_parsed = sse_parsed,
                request = request,
                frontend = frontend,
            }

            local messages_snapshot = tables.shallow_copy(request.body.messages or {})
            -- FYI it is possible the distill in AgentsFrontend has a difference that you need to keep, if so then call save_trace from that spot and not here just for AgentsFrontend (find a way to pass last_sse, that's the only complexity)
            table.insert(messages_snapshot, accum)
            vim.schedule(function()
                M.save_trace(request, frontend, messages_snapshot, sse_parsed, {})
            end)
        end
    end

    M.log_chat_completion_sse()
end

---@param request CurlRequest|CurlRequestForTrace
---@param frontend StreamingFrontend
---@param messages_snapshot? table[]
---@param last_sse table
function M.save_trace(request, frontend, messages_snapshot, last_sse, trace_data)
    local save_dir, trace_id = M.log_request_with(request, frontend)
    -- FYI if this doesn't exist before the save_trace io write happens, the io write will fail silently
    vim.fn.mkdir(save_dir, "p")

    local path = save_dir .. "/" .. trace_id .. "-trace.json"
    -- log:info("trace path", path)
    local file = io.open(path, "w")
    if file then
        local request_body_copy = tables.shallow_copy(request.body)
        -- this way I can avoid issues with timing and AgentsFrontend modifying request.body.messages to add its distilled version of the assistant message
        -- TODO FYI it is confusing that you add the new message to the messages collection that is marked as request_body.messages... maybe you should just store them separately? and nuke request_body.messages to avoid duplication? like top-level messages?
        if messages_snapshot ~= nil then
            -- PRN move this out to caller of save_trace? use trace_data object?
            request_body_copy.messages = messages_snapshot
        end
        trace_data = trace_data or {}
        trace_data.request_body = request_body_copy
        -- last_sse has:
        --   .timings (top-level and under .__verbose.timings)
        --   .__verbose.(prompt, generation_settings)
        --   .__verbose.content (generated raw outputs, but ONLY for stream=false)
        trace_data.last_sse = last_sse

        file:write(json.encode(trace_data, { indent = true }))
        file:close()
    end
end

return M

```

Now the flow is:
1. `log_raw_completion_sse()` — checks `sse_parsed.content ~= nil` internally; returns early if not a raw completion
2. `log_chat_completion_sse()` — proceeds normally for chat completions
3. Both are always called at the bottom; each handles its own guard
messages.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to refactor the `log_sse_to_request` function in `completion_logger.lua`. Currently, it defines two inner functions (`log_raw_completion_sse` and `log_chat_completion_sse`) and then conditionally calls one or the other at the bottom.

The user wants me to:
1. Move the `is_raw_completion` check INSIDE the `log_raw_completion_sse` function itself (or rather, restructure so the check happens within each function's logic)


2. Have both functions run together at the bottom of the outer function instead of conditionally calling one or the other

Looking at the current structure, the condition `local is_raw_completion = sse_parsed.content ~= nil` determines which function gets called. The user's feedback suggests they want both functions to execute at the end, with the raw completion check happening inside each function's own logic rather than gating the entire function call.

Restructuring this means moving the conditional logic into `log_raw_completion_sse` so it can handle both raw and chat completion cases, while `log_chat_completion_sse` continues processing its own logic. Both functions would then be invoked at the bottom of `log_sse_to_request`, allowing them to each determine whether they should act based on the SSE parsed data.

The real issue is that `log_raw_completion_sse` is currently designed for raw completions but gets called for everything. I need to add the `is_raw_completion` check inside that function so it only processes the raw completion logic when appropriate, then call both functions at the end of the outer function. I'm seeing the structure now—both functions need to be called unconditionally at the end, but they should each check their own conditions to determine if they should actually execute. The `log_raw_completion_sse` function would verify it's dealing with a raw completion by checking `sse_parsed.content ~= nil`, while `log_chat_completion_sse` would handle the chat-specific logic. This way the outer function delegates to both, and each inner function decides whether to proceed based on its own criteria.

The user wants me to apply the refactoring I just described to the file `lua/ask-openai/logs/completion_logger.lua`.

Here's the plan based on my previous response:
1. Modify `log_raw_completion_sse` to contain its own `is_raw_completion` check.
2. Remove the explicit `is_raw_completion` check from the main function body.
3. Call both `M.log_raw_completion_sse()` and `M.log_chat_completion_sse()` at the bottom.

Let's do the actual file editing using `run_process` to edit the file with a Lua script or `sed`. Actually, since it's a specific block replacement, I'll just use a python script or `sed` to modify the file. Or better, I'll write the new file content using a `run_process` command with `cat` or just use `sed`.

Wait, I can just use `run_process` to run a small lua script to do the string replacement, or I can use `sed` or `perl`. Let's use `perl` for reliable multi-line replacement, or just `python3`.

Actually, it's easier to just generate the full new file content and write it.
Let's read the current file content, apply the changes, and write it back.

```lua
    if request.type ~= "" then
        -- add `agents/` or `fim/` or `rewrite/` intermediate path

    end
    local trace_id = tostring(request.start_time)
    if frontend.trace then
        -- multi-turn traces use trace's start_time

end

---@param sse_parsed table
---@param request CurlRequest|CurlRequestForTrace
---@param frontend StreamingFrontend
function M.log_sse_to_request(sse_parsed, request, frontend)
    local is_last_sse = sse_parsed.timings -- timings only on last sse

    function M.log_raw_completion_sse()
        -- * /v1/completions endpoint
        -- raw as in there is no chat template that is used to transform messages => raw prompt
        -- instead you provide the raw prompt in the request
        --
        -- NOTE: llama.cpp /completions endpoint works with raw prompt and response (content)
        -- llama.cpp /completions SSEs are split: early SSEs have { content: "...", stop: false }
        -- and the final SSE has { stop: true, timing: true, timings: {...} } WITHOUT content.
        -- So we accumulate content across events, just like the chat endpoint does for delta.content.
        --
        -- TODO modify chat viewers to support this format (.content .request_body.prompt) => can even support raw prompt on regular chat completions too if .__verbose.prompt is present
        --  if no messages => if prompt => show raw prompt, if content => show raw content
        --    add model specific syntax prompt highligting ... at least color key tags like <im_start> in basic chat formats
        --    could lookup on model id/name
        --
        --  TODO look into llama-server option to return .__verbose.content with raw completion even on chat completions and even on streaming responses!
        --     OR tag it onto my verbose flag that I already read off of request body!
        local accum = request.accum or {}
        request.accum = accum

        if sse_parsed.content ~= nil and sse_parsed.content ~= vim.NIL then
            accum.content = (accum.content or "") .. sse_parsed.content
        end

        if is_last_sse then
            M.last_done = {
                sse_parsed = sse_parsed,
                request    = request,
                frontend   = frontend,
            }
            local trace_data = {
                -- put content on trace_data top-level
                content = accum.content,
            }
            vim.schedule(function()
                local no_messages = nil -- b/c not chat endpoint
                M.save_trace(request, frontend, no_messages, sse_parsed, trace_data)
            end)

    end

    log:info("sse_parsed", vim.inspect(sse_parsed))

    -- Replace the original block with a call to the new helper:
    local is_raw_completion = sse_parsed.content ~= nil -- instead of sse_parsed.choices
    if is_raw_completion then
        M.log_raw_completion_sse()
        return
    end

    function M.log_chat_completion_sse()
        -- * /v1/chat/completions endpoint llama-server
        -- FYI delta is the part that changes per SSE, except for last SSE which sets other fields like finish_reason
        -- * key parts (in order):
        --
        -- first delta has role (not split apart on multiple SSEs)
        -- delta = { content = vim.NIL, role = "assistant" },
        --
        -- reasoning_content SSEs
        -- delta = { reasoning_content = "User" },
        --
        -- content SSEs
        -- delta = { content = "---@" },
        --
        -- last SSE choices:
        -- choices = { { delta = vim.empty_dict(), finish_reason = "stop", index = 0 } },
        -- accum the full message (from streaming SSEs) so I can log for all frontends here
        local accum = request.accum or {}
        request.accum = accum

        local choices = sse_parsed.choices
        if not choices then
            return
        end
        local first = choices[1]
        if not first then
            return
        end
        local delta = first.delta
        if not delta then
            return
        end

        if delta.role then
            accum.role = delta.role
        end
        if delta.reasoning_content and delta.reasoning_content ~= vim.NIL then
            accum.reasoning_content = (accum.reasoning_content or "") .. delta.reasoning_content
        end
        if delta.content and delta.content ~= vim.NIL then
            accum.content = (accum.content or "") .. delta.content
        end
        if first.finish_reason and first.finish_reason ~= vim.NIL then
            accum.finish_reason = first.finish_reason
        end
        -- PRN track tool call deltas too? on the response (for output.json)?
        --   FYI request.body, on next turn, already has the tool call
        --   so for now, this is not urgent to add to logs here... I can grab trace logs for after model responds to tool call result
        if is_last_sse then
            -- store for convenient access in-memory, that way if smth fails on save I can still see it here
            M.last_done = {
                sse_parsed = sse_parsed,
                request = request,
                frontend = frontend,
            }

            local messages_snapshot = tables.shallow_copy(request.body.messages or {})
            -- FYI it is possible the distill in AgentsFrontend has a difference that you need to keep, if so then call save_trace from that spot and not here just for AgentsFrontend (find a way to pass last_sse, that's the only complexity)
            table.insert(messages_snapshot, accum)
            vim.schedule(function()
                M.save_trace(request, frontend, messages_snapshot, sse_parsed, {})
            end)
        end
    end

    M.log_chat_completion_sse()
end
...
```

```python
import re

file_path = "~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/logs/completion_logger.lua"
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Old block to replace in log_raw_completion_sse
old_raw_start = """    function M.log_raw_completion_sse()
        -- * /v1/completions endpoint"""
new_raw_start = """    function M.log_raw_completion_sse()
        -- /v1/completions endpoint — raw prompt, no chat template
        local is_raw_completion_sse = sse_parsed.content ~= nil
        if not is_raw_completion_sse then
            return
        end

        -- * /v1/completions endpoint"""

# Remove the manual check block
manual_check = """    log:info("sse_parsed", vim.inspect(sse_parsed))

    -- Replace the original block with a call to the new helper:
    local is_raw_completion = sse_parsed.content ~= nil -- instead of sse_parsed.choices
    if is_raw_completion then
        M.log_raw_completion_sse()
        return
    end

    function M.log_chat_completion_sse()"""

new_check = """    log:info("sse_parsed", vim.inspect(sse_parsed))

    function M.log_chat_completion_sse()"""

# Update the bottom calls
old_bottom = """    M.log_chat_completion_sse()
end"""
new_bottom = """    M.log_raw_completion_sse()
    M.log_chat_completion_sse()
end"""

content = content.replace(old_raw_start, new_raw_start)
content = content.replace(manual_check, new_check)
content = content.replace(old_bottom, new_bottom)

with open(file_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
print("Done")
```

{"command_line":"python3 << 'PYEOF'
import os
fp = '~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/logs/completion_logger.lua'
with open(fp, 'r') as f: c = f.read()
c = c.replace(
    '    function M.log_raw_completion_sse()\n        -- * /v1/completions endpoint',
    '    function M.log_raw_completion_sse()\n        -- /v1/completions endpoint\n        local is_raw_completion_sse = sse_parsed.content ~= nil\n        if not is_raw_completion_sse then\n            return\n        end\n\n        -- * /v1/completions endpoint'
)
c = c.replace(
    '    log:info("sse_parsed", vim.inspect(sse_parsed))\n\n    -- Replace the original block with a call to the new helper:\n    local is_raw_completion = sse_parsed.content ~= nil -- instead of sse_parsed.choices\n    if is_raw_completion then\n        M.log_raw_completion_sse()\n        return\n    end\n\n    function M.log_chat_completion_sse()',
    '    log:info("sse_parsed", vim.inspect(sse_parsed))\n\n    function M.log_chat_completion_sse()'
)
c = c.replace(
    '    M.log_chat_completion_sse()\nend',
    '    M.log_raw_completion_sse()\n    M.log_chat_completion_sse()\nend'
)
with open(fp, 'w') as f: f.write(c)
print('Refactored successfully.')
PYEOF"}

Refactored and committed ✅

- Moved `is_raw_completion_sse` check inside `log_raw_completion_sse`

function.description: Count to, for testing progress and cancellation
function.name: count

recursion_limit.description: LangChain's max messages for the subagent before stopping. Each subagent tool call produces 2 messages (input + output). Leave unset unless the task needs more or fewer than the default (50).

agent_type.description: which subagent profile to use, options: assistant, web-researcher, command-runner, file-finder, test-finder

description.description: describe the task for the agent to perform

function.description: Delegate to a subagent to perform relevent tasks and summarize findings.
function.name: delegate

argv.description: Executable mode: directly spawn a process. argv[0] is the executable, followed by arguments passed verbatim (no shell interpretation). Cannot be combined with 'command_line'.

timeout_ms.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds, defaults to 30,000ms

stdin_text.description: Optional text written to STDIN (written fully, then closed). Useful for heredoc-style input or file contents.

command_line.description: Shell mode: a shell command line executed via the system's default shell. Supports pipes, redirects, globbing. Cannot be combined with 'argv'.

function.description: Run a process on this darwin machine
function.name: run_process

max_chars.description: Maximum number of characters to return

start_index.description: Start at this character offset, useful if a previous fetch was truncated and more context is required

raw.description: Do not convert HTML to markdown

function.description: Fetch a URL and extract HTML contents as markdown
function.name: fetch

lua.description: Lua code to execute. Should be a valid Lua chunk.

function.name: run_in_neovim
function.description: Execute Lua code in the current Neovim process (that hosts your agent). For example, call vim.cmd()

embed_top_k.description: number of embeddings matches to consider for reranking

query.description: query text, what you are looking for

filetype.description: limit matches to a vim compatible filetype. default includes all filetypes

instruct.description: instructions for the query, explain the type of query

top_k.description: number of matches to return (post reranking)

function.name: semantic_grep
function.description: Retrieval tool (the R in RAG) for code and documents in the current workspace. Uses a vector store with embeddings of the entire codebase. And a re-ranker for sorting results.
